mappings.evidence: Opens the session with welcoming language, introduces all panelists by name ('Kaja Kallas...Prince Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud...Major General Pedro Arnulfo Sánchez Suárez...Ambassador Michael Waltz'), asks questions throughout, manages time and audience Q&A, never makes sustained policy arguments. Consistent with bio describing her as a frequent high-profile panel moderator known for crisp questioning.

Addressed directly as 'Kaja' by moderator at turn 18 ('I'll give that to Kallas'), speaks consistently about accountability in the international system, equality of states, European perspective on rules-based order, and critiques the Board of Peace charter for not reflecting UN resolution language. Content matches bio: EU chief diplomat, outspoken on rules-based order and multilateralism. Also referenced in audience question at turn 42 ('And the same question really to Kaja Kallas').

mappings.identified_name: Prince Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud
mappings.evidence: Addressed directly as 'Prince Faisal' by moderator at turns 2 and 36. At turn 33 speaker_c self-introduces as 'Mustafa Barghouti from Palestine' — this turn is reassigned (see flags). Remaining turns reflect Saudi/Gulf diplomatic perspective on the rules-based order, acknowledgment that might-makes-right has been a reality for developing world for decades, and engagement on Gaza/Palestinian self-determination consistent with Saudi FM role. Moderator at turn 58 says 'I'm going to start with you, Prince Faisal' and speaker_c responds at turn 59.

Addressed at turn 6 as 'Minister Sánchez' and introduces himself as speaking on behalf of President Gustavo Petro of Colombia at turn 7. Discusses Colombia's drug fight, Plan Colombia, the Trump-Petro meeting, and Latin American security issues. Content matches bio exactly. Moderator closes at turn 60: 'Thank you, Minister Sánchez.'

Addressed at turn 10 as 'Ambassador Waltz' by moderator. Speaks about UN reform, the Board of Peace, Gaza ceasefire/humanitarian aid, NATO burden-sharing, US leadership in the Western Hemisphere, and references his former congressional constituents in Florida — all consistent with his bio as US Ambassador to the UN and former Florida congressman. Brings a 'MUNGA' hat as a prop at turn 17 ('I have one for you, Lanny' — mishearing moderator's name). Audience member at turn 40 addresses him as 'Ambassador Waltz'.
Self-introduces at turn 29: 'My name is Amal Moudali. I'm a researcher at Princeton University.' Not on the roster.
Self-introduces at turn 40: 'Kaja Napiecka, I'm the chair of the Warsaw Security Forum, Poland.' Not on the roster.

flags.original_attribution: Prince Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud
flags.reason: Turn 33 is a self-introduction as 'Mustafa Barghouti from Palestine' and contains questions directed at the panel from the floor. This is inconsistent with Prince Faisal being the speaker. Mustafa Barghouti is not on the roster and is a distinct audience member from those already identified.

Turns 20 ('Sure.'), 22 ('There is—'), and 50 ('I'm not—') are very short interjections that occur mid-debate between speaker_e (Waltz) and speaker_b (Kallas) turns. Turn 50 in particular mirrors speaker_e's simultaneous 'I'm not—' at turn 51, suggesting diarization confusion between speaker_d and speaker_e labels on brief overlapping speech. These turns do not clearly belong to Sánchez and may be misattributed fragments.

flags.note: Turns 20, 22, and 50 attributed to speaker_d appear to be diarization fragments from the speaker_e/speaker_b exchange rather than genuine Sánchez turns. Turn 50 ('I'm not—') is nearly identical to Waltz's turn 51 opener, suggesting a possible split of a single overlapping utterance across two labels.
